briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the bankruptcy of fintech middleware company Synapse, highlighting risks in the banking-as-a-service model.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple has acquired UK-based open banking startup Credit Kudos, signalling the tech giant's growing interest in financial data and credit services.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep-dive analysis exploring how open banking frameworks are reshaping competition in the payments landscape globally.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News* — Fintech innovation is pushing credit card products in unexpected directions, with new reward structures, embedded features, and niche offerings proliferating across the market.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Canada formally labelled screen scraping as insecure and announced an open banking implementation target, a foundational moment for the country's consumer-driven banking journey.

- **[Anthropic's latest AI model is sparking fears from cybersecurity experts and the banking sector. Here's why.](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/mythos-anthropic-ai-explainer-9.7171597?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Anthropic's newest AI model is raising alarms among cybersecurity professionals and financial institutions over its potential for misuse in fraud and cyberattacks.

- **[Canada's annual inflation rate rose to 2.4% in March, driven by high gas prices](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/march-2026-inflation-rate-9.7170077?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Canada's inflation ticked up to 2.4% in March 2026, with elevated gas prices a key driver, keeping pressure on the Bank of Canada's rate outlook.

- **[Former Startup TNT leader launches coalition for Canada's early-stage investors](https://betakit.com/former-startup-tnt-leader-launches-coalition-for-canadas-early-stage-investors/)** — *BetaKit* — A new coalition aimed at supporting Canada's early-stage investor community has been launched, with implications for fintech and tech startup funding across the country.

- **[Canada's new US economic advisory committee draws backlash from tech leaders](https://betakit.com/canadas-new-us-economic-advisory-committee-draws-backlash-from-tech-leaders/)** — *BetaKit* — Prime Minister Carney's newly announced Canada-U.S. trade advisory council is facing criticism from Canadian tech and innovation leaders concerned about representation and priorities.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Fintech dystopia](https://fintechdystopia.com/)** — *Hacker News* — A critical look at the darker consequences of fintech's rapid expansion, touching on financial exclusion, surveillance, and the risks of over-reliance on algorithmic financial systems — themes directly relevant to building an inclusive and responsible fintech ecosystem in Atlantic Canada.

- **[Canada's AI regulation will be "airtight" on bias, racism, and hate, Solomon says](https://betakit.com/canadas-ai-regulation-will-be-airtight-on-bias-racism-and-hate-solomon-says/)** — *BetaKit* — Canada's forthcoming AI regulatory framework is set to take a firm stance on algorithmic bias and discrimination, with significant implications for AI-driven fintech products serving diverse and underserved communities in Atlantic Canada.
